metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_1117
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Kainate receptor GluR5 (=GRIK1).

rule: NAS_MFCC-BP
rationale:
Protein has ligand-gated ion channel receptor activity and is localized in the presynaptic membrane (shown with high-resolution technique). The inference to term ligand-gated ion channel activity involved in regulation of presynaptic membrane potential (GO:0099507) is trivial and non-controversial.
Pinf_CC:
[P22756] SyNGO ID 1114 (PMID:16360275, EM). Experimental description: Isolation and expression of GluR-5 clones
ForPCR (MullisandFalooona, 1987),oligonucleotideprimersandreaction conditionswereasdescribed(Keinanenetal.,1990).RatforebraincDNA was used as a template. The PCR product (- 500 bp) was gel-purified, cleaved with EcoRI and KpnI, subcloned into doubly cut M13mpl9 RF- DNA (Yanisch-Perronetal.,1985)andsequenced(Sangeretal.,1977). A PCR-generatedDNA fragmentofaGluR-5specificM 13subclonewas internalylabelledwith[3P]dCTPtoaspecificactivityof2x 109/Igand usedasaprobetoscreenaratforebraincDNA libraryconstructedinX- Zap (Stratagene). Five independent cDNAs from these screens were subclonedintopBluescriptSK plasmidvectors(Stratagene,CA) orM13mpl8 andmpl9RF-DNA (Yanisch-Perronetal.,1985)andthesesubcloneswere used for sequence analysis (Sanger et al., 1977). Expression constructs of GluR-5-2 specifying different C-termini were engineered by subcloning 3' BamnHI-AoI or BamHI-EcoRI fragments encoding C-termini of GluR-5-2aor-2-c,respectively,anda5'HindLH-BamHI fragmentdirectly intoamammalianexpressionvectorwithaCMV promoter(Gormanetal.,
1990).TheheterogeneityconcerningglutamineandargininecodonsinTM2 wasgeneratedbyexchanginga400bpBspEI-MscIfragmentcoveringTM regionsItoImofeditedandnon-editedGIuR-5cDNA clones. Theconstructs wereusedtotransfecthumanembryonickidneycels293(ATCC CRL 1573)asdescribed(ChenandOkayama, 1987;Pritcheletal.,1989).These were subjected to binding studies and electrophysiology.
